name: Aura of Kineses
description:
You grant yourself and allies within range the ability to transfer the kinetic energy of attacks. Until the spell ends you can use an action to gravitate all attacks against allies within range until the end of your next turn. All allies within range can use their reaction to redirect any attack made against them, or an ally within 5ft of them, to you. Melee attacks will still be able to be made regardless of the effective range of the attack. Attacks made at long range because of this effect aren’t at disadvantage.

**At Higher Levels:** When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the range increases by 5ft for every 2 slot levels above 2nd.

components.material: a small magnet

name: Avasculate
description:
You shoot a black ray of entropic energy from your outstretched hand, on a hit, your foe violently expell blood or similar vital fluids from their skin.

Make a ranged spell attack to strike a target within range. On a hit, the target must make a successful Constitution Saving Throw vs your Spell Save DC, or take Necrotic damage equal to half its total hit points (rounded down), and be stunned until the end of its next turn.

automation.text:
You shoot a black ray of entropic energy from your outstretched hand, on a hit, your foe violently expell blood or similar vital fluids from their skin.

Make a ranged spell attack to strike a target within range. On a hit, the target must make a successful Constitution Saving Throw vs your Spell Save DC, or take Necrotic damage equal to half its total hit points (rounded down), and be stunned until the end of its next turn.

name: Awaken Undead
description:
You strike the onyx embedded in the blade, sending a wave of intelligence into nearby undead.

Up to 6d6 undead under your control within 60ft of you gain 2d4 intelligence, to a max of 18. Such undead gain the armor and weapon proficiencies of the caster. In addition, they gain proficiency in a single skill, and learn one language, to represent the skills they had in life. This effect last until it is dispelled or you lose control of the undead. You may dispel this effect on a single undead under your control as an action.

Casting this spell sacrifices your own life force, a fragment of mind spreading through the undead. The strain on a construct such as a simulacrum kills it instantly, and the spell fails to take effect. You gain 1d4 levels of exhaustion which must be removed naturally (no magic). If you die from this exhaustion, you lose the ability to cast this spell.

components.material: (A ritual knife with an obsidian blade worth at least 25,000 gp infused with the essence of a powerful undead to be quested for and a humanoid brain embedded with onyx worth at least 10,000 gp, which the spell consumes)

You may dispel this effect on a single undead under your control as an action.

Casting this spell sacrifices your own life force, a fragment of mind spreading through the undead. The strain on a construct such as a simulacrum kills it instantly, and the spell fails to take effect. You gain 1d4 levels of exhaustion which must be removed naturally (no magic). If you die from this exhaustion, you lose the ability to cast this spell.

name: Backbiter
description:
The weapon you indicate during the spell's casting briefly shimmers with a black aura that disappears in an eyeblink.

When you cast this spell, choose one melee weapon within range. The next time a creature uses this weapon to make a melee attack, they have advantage on their attack roll and then the weapon turns on its wielder, provided the spell hasn't ended. The next attack afterward that hits a creature deals damage to the weapon's wielder instead of their target, in addition to the weapon's typical effects. This damage cannot be made non-lethal.

As an action, a creature can attempt an investigation check against your spell save DC when closely analyzing the weapon to find that it was tampered with. As an action, a creature can make an arcana check against your spell save DC to disarm the weapon and end this spell.

Once the weapon attacks its wielder, regardless of whether the attack hits or misses, the spell ends.

**At Higher Levels:**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd or 4th level, this spell lasts up to 8 hours. When you use a spell slot of 5th or 6th level, this spell lasts up to 24 hours. When you use a spell slot of 7th level or higher, this spell lasts until dispelled.

components.material: A dagger

As an action, a creature can attempt an investigation check against your spell save DC when closely analyzing the weapon to find that it was tampered with. As an action, a creature can make an arcana check against your spell save DC to disarm the weapon and end this spell.

Once the weapon attacks its wielder, regardless of whether the attack hits or misses, the spell ends.

name: Beacon
description:
When you cast this spell, target either a creature, object, or location within range. When you do so, choose up to 10 creatures that you are familiar with. If a creature you choose is on the same plane as you, they become aware of the target, and know the most direct path.

While within 1 mile of the target object, the creatures you chose can see a pinpoint of light above the target. This light can be seen through solid objects, except a thin sheet of lead.

When you cast the spell, you may also send a message of 25 words to each of the creatures you choose to be aware of the target. They all receive the same message.

components.material: a piece of glas

automation.text:

While within 1 mile of the target object, the creatures you chose can see a pinpoint of light above the target. This light ca
automation.title: Beacon

name: Boneblade
description:
You dig into your chest and pull out a bone which turns into a magical bladed weapon for the duration.

You take 2d6 bludgeoning damage when you first cast this spell, and each time you make an attack with this weapon, you take 1d6 necrotic damage. This damage cannot be reduced in any way, and bypasses resistance and immunity. If this damage would reduce you to 0 hit points, it reduces you to 1 hit point instead, and the spell ends.

When you cast the spell, choose a mundane melee weapon that deals slashing damage. The bone becomes the chosen weapon, with which you are considered proficient. You can use your spellcasting modifier for the attack and damage rolls with this weapon. This blade deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage on a hit and has advantage on attack rolls made against Good-aligned creatures.

This blade lasts for the duration. If you ever let go of the weapon, it disappears, and you can resummon it to your hand as a bonus action.

name: Breath of Life
description: You transfer your vital essence upon a spectral wind to a creature within range. Choose another creature within range, then spend and roll one of your unspent Hit Dice. The target gains a number of temporary hit points equal to 5 + the number rolled.

components.material: (a small whistle made of petrified wood)

automation.text: You transfer your vital essence upon a spectral wind to a creature within range. Choose another creature within range, then spend and roll one of your unspent Hit Dice. The target gains a number of temporary hit points equal to 5 + the number rolled.

name: Caustic Spell
description:
A viscous and caustic substance spills out across eight 10 foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube.

Each creature in the area when the spill appears must make a dexterity saving throw or take 6d4 acid damage. After which the spill settles, making the spell's area difficult terrain.

For the remainder of the spell's duration, the first time a creature enters the spill on their turn, or starts their turn in the spill, they take 6d4 acid damage. A creature also takes 2d4 acid damage for every 5 feet they move through the spill.

components.material: a citrus fruit

automation.text:
A viscous and caustic substance spills out across eight 10 foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube.

Each creature in the area when the spill appears must make a dexterity saving throw or take 6d4 acid damage. After which the spill settles, making the spell's area difficult terrain.

For the remainder of the spell's duration, the first time a creature enters the spill on their turn, or starts their turn in the spill, they take 6d4 acid damage. A creature also takes 2d4 acid damage for every 5 feet they move through the spill.

name: Devastation
description:
You conjure a ball of concentrated magic energy the size of a soccer ball loudly thrumming with energy, which slowly moves toward a target of your choice, moving through any nonmagical barrier. If it hits a magical barrier made from a spell of 9th level or lower, the orb and the barrier disappear. Otherwise, the orb disappears.

It appears 60ft away from the target at a random location, moving 15ft closer at the beginning of your turn. If the creature touches the orb, they must make a Dexterity Saving Throw or take 20d6+40 force damage on a failure, or take half that much on a successful save. The orb then fades into nothingness.

automation.text:
You conjure a ball of concentrated magic energy the size of a soccer ball loudly thrumming with energy, which slowly moves toward a target of your choice, moving through any nonmagical barrier. If it hits a magical barrier made from a spell of 9th level or lower, the orb and the barrier disappear. Otherwise, the orb disappears.

It appears 60ft away from the target at a random location, moving 15ft closer at the beginning of your turn. If the creature touches the orb, they must make a Dexterity Saving Throw or take 20d6+40 force damage on a failure, or take half that much on a successful save. The orb then fades into nothingness.

name: Divine Reprisal
description: As a bonus action, you can infuse a shield you're currently wielding with divine energy. If the shield is not magical, your AC increases by 1 until you stop wielding the shield or the spell ends. The first time a creature targets you with a melee attack and misses during this spell's duration, your shield bursts with divine radiance as you deflect the blow. The stored energy within releases and deals 1d6 radiant damage to any hostile creatures within 5 feet. When this blast hits an undead or a fiend, it deals an additional 1d6 radiant damage to it. After the blast, the spell ends.

components.material: a shield worth at least 1 sp

name: Ethical Planar Binding
description:
With this spell, you attempt to bind a celestial, an elemental, a fey, or a fiend to the Prime Material Plane for the Duration. The creature must be within range for the entire casting of the spell. At the completion of the casting, the target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is persuaded to stay on this plane for the duration. If the creature was summoned or created by another spell, that spell's duration is extended to match the duration of this spell.

A bound creature is friendly to you and follows your instructions to the best of its ability. You might command the creature to accompany you on an adventure, to guard a location, or to deliver a message. The creature obeys the letter of your instructions. If the creature carries out your instructions completely before the spell ends, it travels to you to report this fact if you are on the same plane of existence. If you are on a different plane of existence, it returns to the place where you bound it and remains there until the spell ends.

**Ethical Caveat**: The binding is a mutual agreement. Should the celestial, elemental. fey, or fiend feel that its binder is acting contrary to its original alignment, or forcing the creature to act in ways that it would not normally wish to do, the summoned creature may make a new CHA save with advantage to immediately teleport back to its own plane of existence. It may do this every round and will not take other actions beyond this save and protecting itself.

components.material: a jewel worth at least 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes

automation.text: With this spell, you attempt to bind a celestial, an elemental, a fey, or a fiend to the Prime Material Plane for the Duration. The creature must be within range for the entire casting of the spell. At the completion of the casting, the target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is persuaded to stay on this plane for the duration. If the creature was summoned or creat
automation.title: Ethical Planar Binding

name: Flame Stride
description:
You gain the ability to enter any source of flame and move from inside it to inside another flame within 500 feet. Both flames must be at least the same size as you. You must use 5 feet of movement to enter the source of fire. You instantly know the location of all other flames within 500 feet and, as part of the move used to enter the flame, can either pass into one of those sources of fire or step out of the fire you're in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination fire, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the flame you entered.

You can use this transportation ability once per round for the duration. You must end each turn outside a flame.

You are temporarily immune to fire damage caused by the flame you're stepping into, but not other sources.

name: Form Undead
description:
This spell must be cast within ten minutes after dusk. You must press the idol used for the material component of this spell to the corpse of a creature with a challenge rating of 10 or lower and hold it there for the duration of the casting of this spell. The corpse must be intact for the duration of this spell’s casting— a destroyed corpse causes this spell to fail.

The corpse reanimates, returning to life and using the statblock of the creature it belonged to, with the following changes:

*   its creature type becomes undead
*   its hit point maximum is halved
*   if it has class levels, it loses all of them
*   it loses any legendary actions and legendary resistances
*   It is unable to cast spells
*   it gains immunity to necrotic damage

As a bonus action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 120 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the creature only defends itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.

An undead raised by this spell is under your control for 24 hours, after which it makes a charisma saving throw. On a failure, you regain control of it for another 24 hours, whereupon it makes the save again. Each time it fails this saving throw, it gains a cumulative +2 bonus to the Charisma save.

On a success, it is hostile to all creatures (including yourself) and is under no compulsion to follow any orders you give it. It has disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws it makes against you (except for the saving throws it makes to break free from your control).

components.material: an intricate black onyx idol inlaid with precious gems worth 10,000gp, which the spell consumes

name: Glacial Shard
description: You form a large ice shard from the moisture of your surroundings to launch at a target that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. If the attack hits, the target takes 2d6 cold damage and the shard shatters into pieces that embed into the target until the start of your next turn. If the target takes an action or bonus action before then, the target takes 1d4 cold damage.

components.material: a piece of pure ice

automation.text: You form a large ice shard from the moisture of your surroundings to launch at a target that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. If the attack hits, the target takes 2d6 cold damage and the shard shatters into pieces that embed into the target until the start of your next turn. If the target takes an action or bonus action before then, the target takes 1d4 cold damage.

name: Glimpse
description:
You can choose a small or smaller object that is familiar to you that isn’t being worn or carried and is on the same plane of existence as you. The spell creates an invisible sensor within 10 feet of the object’s location for the duration. You mentally receive visual information from the sensor, which has normal vision and darkvision out to 30 feet. The sensor allows you to look in every direction. The sensor moves with the object, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. A creature that can see invisible objects sees the sensor as a luminous orb about the size of your fist.

At any time while concentrating on the spell, you can use an action to ping the object’s location. If the object is on the same plane as you, you learn the object’s general direction (but not its distance or any other information).

components.material: a focus worth at least 1,000 gp, such as a crystal ball, a silver mirror, or a font filled with holy water

name: Greater Reincarnate
description:
You touch a willing living humanoid. The spell transforms their body into an adult form of another race. If the target isn’t willing, the spell fails.. The GM rolls a d100 and consults the reincarnate table to determine what form the creature takes when the spell is complete or the GM chooses.

The reincarnated creature recalls its former life and experiences. It retains the capabilities it had in its original form, except it exchanges its original race for the new one and changes its racial traits accordingly.

components.material: rare oils and unguents worth at least 5,000 gp, which the spell consumes

name: Holy Arrow
description:
You conjure and fire a bolt of radiant energy at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 radiant damage. If the target is an undead or fiend, it takes 1d10 radiant damage instead.

This spell's damage increases by one die when you reach 5th level (2d8 or 2d10), 11th level (3d8 or 3d10), and 17th level (4d8 or 4d10).

automation.text:
You conjure and fire a bolt of radiant energy at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 radiant damage. If the target is an undead or fiend, it takes 1d10 radiant damage instead.

This spell's damage increases by one die when you reach 5th level (2d8 or 2d10), 11th level (3d8 or 3d10), and 17th level (4d8 or 4d10).

name: Hot Shot
description:
You brandish the weapon used in the spell’s casting and make a ranged attack with it against one creature within 20 feet of you. On a hit, your walking speed increases by 5 ft until the end of your turn.

This spell’s damage and speed bonus increases when you reach certain levels. At 5th level, the ranged attack deals an extra 1d6 fire damage and your speed increases by 10 feet instead of 5 feet. The damage roll and speed bonus increases by 1d6 and 5 feet at 11th level (2d6 and 15 feet) and 17th level (3d6 and 20 feet).

components.material: a ranged weapon worth at least 1 sp

automation.text:
You brandish the weapon used in the spell’s casting and make a ranged attack with it against one creature within 20 feet of you. On a hit, your walking speed increases by 5 ft until the end of your turn.

This spell’s damage and speed bonus increases when you reach certain levels. At 5th level, the ranged attack deals an extra 1d6 fire damage and your speed increases by 10 feet instead of 5 feet. The damage roll and speed bonus increases by 1d6 and 5 feet at 11th level (2d6 and 15 feet) and 17th level (3d6 and 20 feet).

name: Light Trap
description: This spell creates a mental phantasm of lights and sounds that dull the senses. Choose a point within range. Each creature you choose in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, their vision and hearing are restricted to 30ft for the duration. An effected creature can make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

components.material: a bit of fleece

automation.text: This spell creates a mental phantasm of lights and sounds that dull the senses. Choose a point within range. Each creature you choose in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, their vision and hearing are restricted to 30ft for the duration. An effected creature can make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

name: Magic Toggle
description:
You implant a small repeatable magical mechanical force within a tiny object within range. When you do so, choose two rotational states relative to it's current rotation for the item to switch between. When the spell causes the item to switch between the two rotational states, it does so with the least required movement and can be designated to take anywhere from 1 second to 10 minutes. Finally, determine the circumstance that will trigger the spell to rotate the object.

When the circumstance occurs, the object rotates to the next or previous state (the second state when triggered for the first time). If an outside force or object would prevent the object from rotating, it stops harmlessly and that particular trigger is ignored. When you cast this spell, you can have the spell end after it returns to its original position, or it can remain and repeat its rotation whenever the trigger occurs.

The triggering circumstance can be as general or as detailed as you like, though it must be based on visual or audible conditions that occur within 30 feet of the object. For example, you could instruct a lock to automatically reset its rotation when the door is closed.

components.material: a pinch of iron and cobalt powder worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes

name: Magicked Bolt
description:
You charge a free hand with crackling magical energy. When you cast this spell, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can point at a creature you can see within 60 feet of you and release a bolt, provided the spell hasn't ended. The target must make a constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn.

When you successfully incapacitate a target with this spell, you can instantly teleport to an empty space of your choice within 5 feet of it, and your melee weapon attacks against the incapacitated creature deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

The spell ends early if a target succeeds on its saving throw, or if you use your bonus action to do anything else.

automation.text:

When you cast this spell, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can point at a creature you can see within 60 feet of you and release a bolt, provided the spell hasn't ended. The target must make a constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn.

When you successfully incapacitate a target with this spell, you can instantly teleport to an empty space of your choice within 5 feet of it, and your melee weapon attacks against the incapacitated creature deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

The spell ends early if a target succeeds on its saving throw, or if you use your bonus action to do anything else.

name: Menny's Ephemeral Script
description:
You imbue a piece of parchment, paper, or some other suitable writing material with illusory writing that lasts for the duration. This writing is not suitable for scribing a spell scroll or filling a spellbook.

When you cast this spell, the page is instantly filled with text of your choosing. Choose a duration between 1 minute and 1 year. After this amount of time has elapsed, the magic fades and the writing vanishes.

Any time before the spell ends, you, and any creatures you designate when you cast the spell, can cast it again to extend the duration, up to a maximum of 1 year. Extending the duration in this way does not require the material component.

components.material: enchanted ink worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes

automation.text:
You imbue a piece of parchment, paper, or some other suitable writing material with illusory writing that lasts for the duration. This writing is not suitable for scribing a spell scroll or filling a spellbook.

When you cast this spell, the page is instantly filled with text of your choosing. Choose a duration between 1 minute and 1 year. After this amount of time has elapsed, the magic fades and
automation.title: Menny's Ephemeral Script

name: Moonlight's Shadow
description:
You imbue a melee weapon you touch with moonlight and enchant your own shadow to hinder your foes. Until the spell ends, the weapon emits bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15. If the weapon isn't already a magic weapon, it becomes one for the duration.

When you make a melee weapon attack on your turn, that attack can originate from any space within the lights radius as if you were in that space. You make this choice for each attack, as the moonlight solidifies to attack them.

Once during this spell's duration, you may use an action to fill a 30-foot radius circle, centered on the ground underneath you, with a magical shadow that remains there for the spell's duration. If a creature ends it's turn within this area, you may force them to make a strength saving throw. On a failure they are knocked prone and must use all of their speed to stand back up, rather than half.

components.material: a melee weapon worth at least 1 sp

name: Phantom Coach
description:
A Huge, quasi-real, horse-drawn coach appears on the ground in an unoccupied space of your choice within range. You decide the creature's and coach's appearance, and it is equipped with the necessary gear to pull the carriage. Any of the equipment created by the spell vanishes in a puff of smoke if it is carried more than 10 feet away from the steed or coach.

For the duration, you and up to 4 other creatures you choose can ride in the coach. The steeds uses the Riding Horse stat block, except it has a Speed of 100 feet and can travel 11 miles in an hour. When the spell ends, the steeds and coach gradually fades, giving the riders 1 minute to dismount. The spell ends early if the steed takes any damage.

For the duration, you and up to 4 oth
automation.title: Phantom Coach

name: Power Word Defile
description:
You utter a word of defilement on a creature you see within range. If the creature you choose has 150 hit points of fewer, the target gains vulnerability to all damage that they are not resistant or immune to. Otherwise, the spell has no effect on it.

A defiled creature must make a Constitution Save at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, the creature is no longer defiled.

automation.text:
You utter a word of defilement on a creature you see within range. If the creature you choose has 150 hit points of fewer, the target gains vulnerability to all damage that they are not resistant or immune to. Otherwise, the spell has no effect on it.

A defiled creature must make a Constitution Save at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, the creature is no longer defiled.

name: Programmed Light
description:
You implant a magical light into an object that you touch which activates when a trigger condition is met. Next, determine the circumstances that will trigger the spell to turn on the light.

When that circumstance occurs, the object begins to glow, emitting dim light in a radius of 5 feet around the object. If the trigger condition you specified is a spoken phrase, the object itself becomes colored if the final word of the phrase is a color. Otherwise, the object turns white. When you cast this spell, you can have the spell end after it activates for the first time, or it can remain and continue to change colors if the trigger condition occurs again. If the trigger condition occurs while the light is already on, the object changes color to the new color at the end of a spoken message, or returns to normal and stops glowing if no color is spoken.

The triggering circumstance can be as general or as detailed as you like, though it must be based on visual or audible conditions that occur within 30 feet of the object. For example, you could instruct the object to light up when any creature moves within 30 feet of the object or when a silver bell rings within 30 feet of it.

components.material: crushed glass worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes

automation.text:
You implant a magical light into an object that you touch which activates when a trigger condition is met. Next, determine the circumstances that will trigger the spell to turn on the light.

When that circumstance occurs, the object begins to glow, emitting dim light in a radius of 5 feet around the object. If the trigger condition you specified is a spoken phrase, the object itself becomes color
automation.title: Programmed Light

name: Protection from Elements
description:
You touch a nonmagical object, warding it against the ravages of time and nature. For the duration, the object is immune to natural wear and tear, including weathering, rust, rot, mildew, and other forms of gradual decay. The object resists tarnishing and maintains its structural integrity despite exposure to the elements.

Additionally, the object gains a damage threshold equal to 5 × the level of the spell slot used to cast this spell. An object with a damage threshold has immunity to all damage unless it takes an amount of damage from a single attack or effect equal to or greater than its damage threshold, in which case it takes damage as normal, and the spell ends.

components.material: an amber crystal worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes

name: Rakshasa's Claw
description: Channeling the vile and scheming nature of the Rakshasa, you evoke by splaying three fingers apart and making a downward vertical slash in the air. It can also be done with a horizontal swipe to deal less damage to more targets at once. A target you choose within range must make a Dexterity save or suffer 6d8 magical slashing damage. You can alternatively choose to target any creature in a 20-foot cube originating from yourself, dealing 3d8 magical slashing damage to each target. In either case, on a successful save, each target takes half as much magical slashing damage.

automation.text: Channeling the vile and scheming nature of the Rakshasa, you evoke by splaying three fingers apart and making a downward vertical slash in the air. It can also be done with a horizontal swipe to deal less damage to more targets at once. A target you choose within range must make a Dexterity save or suffer 6d8 magical slashing damage. You can alternatively choose to target any creature in a 20-foot cube originating from yourself, dealing 3d8 magical slashing damage to each target. In either case, on a successful save, each target takes half as much magical slashing damage.

name: Reflection
description:
You create a reflective magical barrier that turns harmful attacks back upon their attackers. For the duration, when a creature hits you with an attack, it takes 2d8 radiant damage.

Additionally, if you fail a saving throw against a spell cast by a creature you can see, they take 1d8 radiant damage + an extra 1d8 for each spell slot level of the spell cast.

components.material: a small sprinkling of powdered silver

name: Roaring Thunder
description: You let out a deafening roar as a shockwave of energy collides with all around you. Each creature within range must succeed on a constitution saving throw or take 5d8 thunder damage and be pushed up to 20 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and is not moved. Regardless of success or failure, a creature within range is deafened for 1 minute. Objects within range also take this damage.

components.material: a small paper cone

automation.text: You let out a deafening roar as a shockwave of energy collides with all around you. Each creature within range must succeed on a constitution saving throw or take 5d8 thunder damage and be pushed up to 20 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and is not moved. Regardless of success or failure, a creature within range is deafened for 1 minute. Objects within range also take this damage.

name: Skeleton Surplus!
description:
One creature you can see within range becomes the target of an onslaught of ethereal skeletons. At the end of each of the target's turns, four skeletons immediately appear in the closest unoccupied spaces to the target and are hostile towards it.

The skeletons' attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunities, and last until either they or the target die, where they disappear leaving no trace. They cannot be commanded and will exclusively take actions harmful to the target, or to aid in harming the target.

If any skeletons remain when more are to be summoned, that many additional skeletons are summoned.

name: Skeleton Surprise
description:
When you cast this spell, target one creature you can see within range. 8d4 skeletons immediately appear in the closest unoccupied spaces to the target, and are hostile towards it. Alternatively, you can delay the appearance of the skeletons by up to an hour.

The skeletons' attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunities, and last until either they or the target die, where they disappear leaving no trace. They cannot be commanded and will exclusively take actions harmful to the target, or to aid in harming the target.

If a creature already under the effects of skeleton surprise is targeted, you instead change the time at which the skeletons appear, rather than causing more to appear. You may also change the time that the skeletons appear as an action, so long as the time you choose falls within one hour of the initial casting.

The casting of this spell does not require verbal or somatic components, hence the surprise.

name: Spirit Ejection
description:
You attempt to forcefully tear the spirit of a creature from its body, leaving it momentarily defenseless. Choose one humanoid creature within range (creatures under the effects of a spell, feature or magic item that wards them against Undead or possession cannot have their spirit ejected by this spell). The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, their spirit is ejected from their body and manifests as a spectral form within 10 feet of them. The target’s body becomes incapacitated, and has disadvantage on saving throws against effects that would possess them. The spirit resembles their mortal form in almost every way, replicating game statistics but not their possessions. Any damage or other effects that apply to your spirit or physical body affects the other. In addition, the spirit is incorporeal, resistant to all damage except psychic or radiant, and it must choose whether it gets a move, an action, or a bonus action on a turn; it gets only one of the three. The spirit is tethered to the body and cannot move more than 60 feet away from it.

At the start of each of the target’s turns, they can attempt a Wisdom saving throw to end the effect early. If they succeed, their spirit returns to their body, and they regain control at the start of their next turn.

If the body is reduced to 0 hit points while the spirit is still ejected, the creature dies instantly. If killed in this way, its spirit detaches from its corpse as a Specter under your control, the statistics of which are in the Monster Manual.

You can mentally command the Specter or any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 60 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one), and in combat the Specter shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours.

The Specter remains in your service for up to 10 days, at which point it vanishes to the afterlife. While the Specter is in your service, its soul is trapped and cannot be revived. A Remove Curse, Dispel Evil and Good, or Wish spell ends this spells effect.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th or 7th level or higher, the target has disadvantage on its initial saving throw. At 8th or 9th level, the target instead rises as a Poltergeist if killed.

components.material: a pinch of bone dust, and a tarnished silver chain

The Specter remains in your service for up to 10 days, at which point it vanishes to the afterlife. Wh

name: Spirit Ward
description:
Weaving a web of necromantic energies around you or another, you are protected against influences upon your soul or animating spirit. You touch a living humanoid or a corpse. For the duration, the target has advantage on death saving throws, and is protected from spells and effects that would allow their soul, spirit or body to be queried, trapped, possessed or manipulated, such as Soul Cage, Speak with Dead, Magic Jar, Spirit Ejection or other related effects.

If the humanoid or corpse is targeted by such an effect, it automatically fails and the enemy takes 4d10 psychic damage, as the Ward lashes out to protect them.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the duration is increased by 5 days and the psychic damage is increased by 1d10 for each level beyond 5th.

components.material: a silver chain mesh worth at least 1,500+ GP

name: Tenser's Unbound Transformation
description:
You endow yourself with endurance and martial prowess fueled by magic. Until the spell ends, you can't cast spells of 6th level or higher, and you gain two of the following benefits:

*   You gain 50 temporary hit points. If any of these remain when the spell ends, they are lost.
*   You have advantage on attack rolls that you make with simple and martial weapons.
*   When you hit a target with a weapon attack, that target takes an extra 2d12 force damage.
*   You have proficiency with shields, simple weapons, and martial weapons.
*   You have proficiency in Strength and Constitution saving throws.
*   You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your - turn. You ignore this benefit if you already have a feature, like Extra Attack, that gives you extra attacks.

Immediately after the spell ends, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or suffer one level of exhaustion.

components.material: a few hairs from a bull

automation.text:
You endow yourself with endurance and martial prowess fueled by magic. Until the spell ends, you can't cast spells of 6th level or higher, and you gain two of the following benefits:

*   You gain 50 temporary hit points. If any of these remain when the spell ends, they are lost.
*   You have advantage on attack rolls that you make with simple and martial weapons.
*   When you hit a target with a weapon attack, that target takes an extra 2d12 force damage.
*   You have proficiency with shields, simple weapons, and martial weapons.
*   You have proficiency in Strength and Constitution saving throws.
*   You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your - turn. You ignore this benefit if you already have a feature, like Extra Attack, that gives you extra attacks.

Immediately after the spell ends, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or suffer one level of exhaustion.

name: Thought Forms
description:
Through carefully crafted thought projections and the transmutation of mental energies, you create a field of potentiality around you, exceeding your normal limitations. When you cast this spell you create 6 Thought Forms. You can consume these Thought Forms in a number of ways, granting additional benefits depending on your choice:

*   When you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you misses with an attack, you can consume any number of Thought Form. They gain a +2 bonus to the attack roll equal to the number of Thought Forms consumed, potentially turning the miss into a hit.
*   Once per turn, when you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you take the Attack action, you can consume 2 Thought Forms to allow them to make an additional attack as part of that same action.
*   When you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you take damage from an attack, you can consume any number of Thought Forms as a reaction, granting them 10 temporary hit points for each Thought Form consumed. These temporary hit points last until the start of your next turn.
*   When you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, you can use your reaction to consume any number of your Thought Forms. They gain a +2 bonus to your saving throw for each Thought Form consumed, potentially turning the failure into a success.

As a bonus action on your turn, you can expend an unused spell slot to create a number of Thought Forms equal to the level of the spell slot expended, up to a maximum of 6.

components.material: (a gemstone worth at least 5,000gp, which the spell consumes)

name: Toxic Spike
description: You shoot a thin toxic spike at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. If it hits, the target takes 2d8 poison damage, and the spike is lodged in the creature’s body. At the start of each of the creature’s turns while the spike is embedded in the creature, the target takes 1d6 poison damage. The target may use their action to pull the spike out, after which the spell ends.

components.material: a tiny metal spike

automation.text: You shoot a thin toxic spike at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. If it hits, the target takes 2d8 poison damage, and the spike is lodged in the creature’s body. At the start of each of the creature’s turns while the spike is embedded in the creature, the target takes 1d6 poison damage. The target may use their action to pull the spike out, after which the spell ends.

name: Transdimensional Expansion
description:
You magically expand the dimensions of an existing container, room, or otherwise fully enclosed space beyond its physical limits. Select an existing physical space (no larger than 125 sq ft.) with at most one single point of entry such as a lid, door, window or flap, (no larger than 10 sq ft), and with a solid border (no transparent walls, windows, etc.). As a part of this spell, the caster must inscribe runes around the opening and the borders of the space to maintain its external structure. The interior dimensions of that space expand by up to 5 feet in any direction that you choose as the spell takes effect, warping to accommodate any items or creatures inside.

When the spell ends, the space returns to its normal size and any items or occupants unable to fit in the original space are softly ejected through the opening. If there is no opening or the objects cannot fit through it, the items or creatures are scattered randomly in the Astral Plane as determined by the GM.

Casting this spell in the same space every day for 30 days makes the effect permanent; further castings can be used to expand an existing Transdimensional Space beyond its size provided that it still has a single exterior border with no more than one point of entry.

components.material: 1,000 gp worth of Residuum or any Arcane Dust/Powder

name: True Awaken
description:
You awaken the mind of a creature, granting it sentience and self-awareness. Choose one non-hostile creature you touch.

You can have a number of creatures awakened by this spell equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum of one). If casting this spell would exceed your limit, the creature awakened longest ago immediately loses all benefits of True Awaken.

**Restrictions:**

This spell automatically fails if the target is summoned or maintained by a concentration spell, is planar bound, has Legendary Resistance, or has an Intelligence score of 10 or higher.

**Effects:**

If the spell succeeds, the target’s Intelligence becomes 10. The target gains the ability to understand one language you know and becomes capable of basic reasoning, learning, and self-awareness.

**Limitations:**

This spell grants no combat benefits, proficiencies, spellcasting, or CR increases. It does not remove or alter magical control already affecting the creature, and it does not restore lost memories or identity.

components.material: clay and runes worth 2,000 gp, consumed

name: True Reincarnate
description:
You touch a willing living humanoid. The spell transforms their body into an adult form of another race. If the target isn’t willing, the spell fails. The caster can choose a medium or small humanoid race for the humanoid to transform into, but must have a vial of willing given blood from a member of that race.

The reincarnated creature recalls its former life and experiences. It retains the capabilities it had in its original form, except it exchanges its original race for the new one and changes its racial traits accordingly.

components.material: rare oils and unguents worth at least 10,000 gp, which the spell consumes, and vial of willingly given blood from the race to be reincarnated into

name: Vexatious Venom
description:
With an upward flourish of your hand, a splash of poisonous venom is released from your hand into the face of a creature in range. The targeted creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn on a failed save.

The spell’s damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

automation.text:
With an upward flourish of your hand, a splash of poisonous venom is released from your hand into the face of a creature in range. The targeted creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn on a failed save.

The spell’s damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

name: Virulent Infection
description:
You attempt infect a creature nearby with a nasty strain of poison. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 3d8 poison damage and be infected. An infected creature is poisoned and takes an additional 2d8 poison damage at the start of each of their turns. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much poison damage and is not infected. The spell has no effect on creatures immune to the poisoned condition.

When an infected creature dies, they explode in a poisonous burst. Each creature within 5 feet of the infected creature when it dies must roll a constitution saving throw or be infected as well, suffering the full effects of the spell.

At the end of each of each infected creature’s turns until the spell ends, they can make another Constitution saving throw. On a success, the spell ends for that creature.

components.material: a splash of weak poison

automation.text:
You attempt infect a creature nearby with a nasty strain of poison. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 3d8 poison damage and be infected. An infected creature is poisoned and takes an additional 2d8 poison damage at the start of each of their turns. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much poison damage and is not infected. The spell has no effect on creatures immune to the poisoned condition.

When an infected creature dies, they explode in a poisonous burst. Each creature within 5 feet of the infected creature when it dies must roll a constitution saving throw or be infected as well, suffering the full effects of the spell.

At the end of each of each infected creature’s turns until the spell ends, they can make another Constitution saving throw. On a success, the spell ends for that creature.

name: Warp Ward
casttime: 1 reaction (when a creature within 5 ft of you hits you with an attack)
description: The attacker must make a constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 ft of you. If the result of this teleport places the target in a location where the attack would no longer be in range, it misses.

automation.text: The attacker must make a constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 ft of you. If the result of this teleport places the target in a location where the attack would no longer be in range, it misses.

name: Weighted Weapon
description: A weapon you touch becomes heavier on impact towards enemies. For the duration, the weapon becomes magical, if it isn't already, and deals one extra die of the weapon's damage die.

name: Withering Smite
description: Your weapon radiates terrible energy that empowers the next strike you make with it. The next time you hit with a melee weapon attack during this spell’s duration, your attack deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. If the target of that attack was a creature, it must make a successful Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute, its flesh rotting around the wound. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. The rotting effect also ends if the affected creature receives magical healing.

automation.text: Your weapon radiates terrible energy that empowers the next strike you make with it. The next time you hit with a melee weapon attack during this spell’s duration, your attack deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. If the target of that attack was a creature, it must make a successful Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute, its flesh rotting around the wound. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. The rotting effect also ends if the affected creature receives magical healing.
